title: salix × hippophaeifolia subsp. trevirani
intro: Salix × hippophaeifolia subsp. trevirani, also known as the Treviran willow or hybrid willow, is a plant that belongs to the willow family and is a hybrid of Salix alba and Salix viminalis. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in salicin, a compound that provides pain relief and reduces fever, making it beneficial for treating headaches, sore throats, and menstrual cramps. Salix × hippophaeifolia subsp. trevirani is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system.
